fields.description:
## Why
The code-review gate cast ZERO 'BLOCK — finding' votes across 162 changes since the code-v2 impact model landed, while 76% of changes carried advisories and at least three carried confirmed must-fix findings (#529 impact 0.0 — proved out on main within the hour; #518 importlib code-execution at 0.54; #539 correctness at 0.60). Observed impact distribution clusters at {0, 0.32, 0.36, 0.54, 0.60} with an apparent 0.60 ceiling — the 0.95 default threshold is unreachable. Before enabling blocking we need the empirically-correct threshold, not a guess: the operator's decision rule is the crossover point at which a finding is MORE LIKELY correctly-blocking than false-blocking (0.6 is the prior/starting hypothesis).

## What
- Assemble the post-2026-07-08 code-v2 corpus: the ~149 deduplicated code-review finding records in the ticket store (.tickets-tracker). NOTE on schema: code-review sidecars use `schema: code_review_result_v1` and store findings under `blocking`/`advisory`/`coaching` buckets (each with an `impact`), keyed by `target_ticket`/`change_fingerprint` — NOT the plan-review `plan_review_result_v1` shape (`findings`/`material_fingerprint`). Join to their Gerrit changes and post-merge history (follow-up fix commits, red-main incidents).
- Adjudicate each finding with impact >= 0.3 as block-worthy vs not (blind to its score where feasible), mirroring the b463 plan-review methodology (manual scoring of the would-block slice; Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale).
- Compute precision over the SAME decision quantity the gate actually thresholds on — NOT raw impact. The code-review block decision (pass3_decide/decide.py) compares a finding's PRIORITY = validity × impact against `block_threshold`, so the derivation computes precision(block-worthy | priority >= t) across candidate t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6}; select the SMALLEST t where precision > 0.5, tie-broken toward fewer false blocks. (Recording raw-impact percentiles too is fine for transparency, but the derived threshold must be in priority units so b9c0 can consume it directly.) Explicitly analyze the 0.54 band (the #518 security findings) and whether the 0.60 ceiling means t=0.6 only ever catches max-priority findings.
- DERIVATION TOOLING (corrected): `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` reads the PLAN-review sidecar schema and CANNOT consume code-review sidecars — do NOT reuse it for this corpus. Instead commit a dedicated code-review derivation script under docs/experiments/ (e.g. `derive_code_review_threshold.py`) that loads the `code_review_result_v1` sidecars (blocking/advisory buckets), joins the adjudication labels, and computes precision(block-worthy | impact >= t) across the candidate thresholds. TWO DISTINCT STEPS (do not conflate them): (1) `docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py` is the impact-MODEL separation gate — it scores the STATIC `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` HIGH/NIT fixture and gates that the code-v2 impact model separates HIGH from NIT (the ADR-0036 model-quality prerequisite). It does NOT derive a threshold from the real corpus. (2) the THRESHOLD is derived by the new `derive_code_review_threshold.py` over the real `code_review_result_v1` sidecar corpus (precision-crossover). Run (1) to confirm the model is sound, then use (2) to pick the smallest t with precision > 0.5. If extending the existing calibrator is preferred over a new script, its loader must first be taught the code-review schema and that documented — but the default is the standalone code-review script.

## Scope
Analysis + a written threshold recommendation committed as a REPO ARTIFACT under docs/experiments/ (e.g. `derive_code_review_threshold.md` or the script's `--emit-recommendation` output file) recording the chosen threshold + the precision-crossover / 0.54-band / 0.60-ceiling analyses (a ticket comment may mirror it, but the committed file is the verifiable artifact); NO routing/config change here (that is the sibling enablement story).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The adjudicated corpus is committed under docs/experiments/ (e.g. `code_review_adjudication.jsonl`) with the derivation script/notebook. SCHEMA (define the value vocabulary): each row is `{finding_id: str, criterion: str, impact: float, validity: float, priority: float (= validity × impact, the exact quantity the gate thresholds on), adjudication: "block-worthy" | "not-block-worthy" | "ambiguous", evidence: str (the follow-up-fix commit / red-main incident / reviewer rationale grounding the call)}`. The derivation computes precision(block-worthy | priority >= t) directly from the `priority` column, so the corpus carries the exact inputs the decision rule consumes (no derived quantity is missing). `adjudication` uses exactly that three-value closed vocabulary (block-worthy = a human would want this to block; not-block-worthy = advisory-appropriate; ambiguous = excluded from the precision computation, counted separately).
- [ ] The chosen threshold and its precision-crossover justification (with the 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses) are committed as a REPO ARTIFACT under docs/experiments/ — CI-verifiable that the file exists and names a numeric threshold (not only a ticket comment).
- [ ] The ADR-0036 A/B gate result (HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline) is recorded and passes.
- [ ] The derivation logic is unit-tested UNDER `tests/` (not only in docs/experiments/, which `make lint`/`make test` do not traverse): add `tests/unit/test_derive_code_review_threshold.py` (or a `scripts`-marked test) that imports the derivation module and asserts the precision-over-priority computation on a tiny in-test fixture — so the logic is covered by `make test`. The script itself is additionally proven by a direct run: `.venv/bin/python docs/experiments/derive_code_review_threshold.py --corpus <path>` producing the recorded threshold.
- [ ] make lint passes repo-wide (ruff config governs whether docs/experiments/ is linted; the committed script conforms to whatever scope applies).
- [ ] make test passes repo-wide, including the new tests/ derivation-logic test.
